ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-22-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: Jane Bradley for and with Ronald Acker (Petitioner).

Ms. Bradley explained that there was no reason for the Public Administrator to retain the $53,500.00 reserve as they did not file any tax returns, that her client filed taxes, and that any tax liabilities would fall on him. The Court accepted Ms. Bradley's explanation, and granted the petition (order for the same filed in open court).

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Jackson.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1093-1095 Plymouth Ave, San Francisco)
Off Calendar Jul-30-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jane Bradley for Ronald Acker (Petitioner).

Ms. Bradley stated that notice had been sent to the Inheritance Funding attorney, not to Mr. Lyons and that notice was also sent to Mr Schroeder. The sale was confirmed at $765,000.00. The Court granted the petition. An additional bond in the amount of $355,000.00 is required.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bonner.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-08-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. JONATHAN FERDON APPEARING FOR THE PUBLIC ADMINISTRATOR;
ATTY. JANE BRADLEY FOR THE PETITIONER

MS. BRADLEY REPORTED TO THE COURT REGARDING HER CONCERN REGARDING THE STATUTORY FEES FOR THE PUBLIC ADMINISTRATOR AND IT'S ATTORNEY'S FEES. THE TOTAL STATUTORY FEES ARE BASED ON THE AMOUNT WHEN THE APPRAISED VALUE OF THE PROPERTY LOCATED AT PLYMOUTH AVENUE WAS STILL HIGH AND NOW THE VALUE OF THE PROPERTY HAD GONE DOWN TO $750,000.00.THE PROPERTY IS CURRENTLY ON THE MARKET AND STILL HAD NO OFFERS. PETITIONER REQUEST THAT THE AMOUNT ON WHICH THE STATUTORY FEE BE BASED AND BE REDUCED BY $205,000.00. MS. BRADLEY IS CONCERNED ALSO ABOUT THE REQUEST TO WITHOLD THE AMOUNT OF $50,000.00 TO OFFSET ANY POSSIBLE FEDERAL OR STATE INCOME TAX LIABILITY. THEY ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR ANY INCOME TAX LIABILITY AAND THERE IS NO ESTATE TAX DUE AND NO ESTATE TAX RETURN WILL BE FILED. MR. FERDON STATED THAT THE $50,000 IS NEEDED FOR THE CAPITAL GAINS TAX. THE PUBLIC ADMINISTRATOR WAS CALLED UPON THIS CASE TO DETERMINE WHERE ASSETS WERE BEING DIVERTED AND IS ON A TREASURE HUNT LOOKING OR SEARCHING FOR ASSETS. THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: JOHN DEARMAN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT/SAJJA RAVINANTAPRICHA REPORTER: KATH BERSAMIN, CSR # 11722

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The San Francisco Public Administrator'S Accounting
Off Calendar Mar-10-2008 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Filing Of Accounting
Off Calendar Nov-07-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jane Bradley for and with Ronald Acker. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA"). William Wong for and with Lawrence Acker.

Ms. Bradley informed the Court that the matter had settled, but they did not have Archie's signature. Lawrence Acker reported that Archie was on the way. Ms. Bradley expressed that she wanted the new administrator to to sign the agreement. The Court passed the matter until Archie's arrival. The matter went back on the record at 11:00 a.m. The Settlement Agreement was read for the record by Ms. Bradley. The Court signed the Order appointing Ronald Acker as the Administrator of the estate setting the bond at $630,000.00. The Court also signed the Settlement Agreement. Mr. Ferdon will do the final account. The status for the final account is set on March 10, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: T. Bonner.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-24-2007 Continued To Nov-07-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Jane Bradley appeared for and with Ronald Acker (Petitioner). Jonathan Ferdon appeared for the San Francisco Public Administrator. William Wong for and with Lawrence Acker (Petitioner).

Ms. Bradley informed the Court that the case went to mediation and the matter had settled. Counsel also requested more time to have all the siblings sign the settlement agreement so that it could be presented to and approved by the Court. The Court was also informed that the agreement included the withdrawal of Lawrence Acker's petition and the appointment of Ronald Acker as the admintrator of the estate. Ms. Bradley requested that Roanld Acker be appointed today. Mr. Ferdon expressed concern reagarding the possibility of the settlement not being finalized, appointing Lawrence Acker prematurely, and suggested continuing the matter for two weeks. Mr Wong stated that he and his client were in agreement with the appointment. Ms. Bradley agreed to the continuance. The Court continued the matter to November 7, 2007 for status of the settlement.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Reporter: Janet Pond CSR#5292. Bailiff: Stella Obi. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Off Calendar Dec-05-2006 Continued To Jan-23-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Jane Bradley appearing for and with Ronald Acker. William Wong appearing for and with Lawrence Acker. Jonathan Ferdon appearing for Public Administrator. Report of Lawrence Acker filed. Ms. Bradley objected to the Report not being an accounting. Court has not seen the Report. Mr. Wong is trying to get more information on the bank accounts. Mr. Ferdon wanted to know where the rents go. Lawrence Acker stated the rents went for payment of the work on the property. Payment of utility bills for both properties. Court wants a completed accounting to clarify the questions. Court is ordering keys to be turn over to Mr. Ferdon. The Special Administrator will be responsible for the properties and picking up the mails. Mr. Wong wants to question Ronald Acker on his knowledge of any trust instrument. Ronald Acker stated he does not have any trust instrument. The Court wants everyone to cooperate with Mr. Ferdon. Lawerence Acker's accounting to start with the date POA was signed until her DOD. All matters are continued to 1/23/07. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Ron Sinclair. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

MINI MINUTES: Jane Bradley appearing for and with Ronald Acker. William Wong appearing for and with Lawrence Acker. Jonathan Ferdon appearing for Public Administrator. No agreement between the parties. Ms. Bradley has requested for an accounting from Lawrence Acker and has not received it. Lawrence wants to fix up the house before the sale to get the best price. Mr. Wong requests for continuance to work on settlement. Mr. Ferdon says the Public Administrator will not fix the house if there is no funds in the estate. Court is appointing the Public Administrator as the Special Administrator with powers to sell. Mr. Ferdon will need information on loans and taxes. The petitioners are to file the accounting and all information on loans and taxes one week prior to the next hearing date. Mr. Ferdon will prepare an order. Both petitions are continued to 12/5.
